commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add network pivoting support: tunnels table, pivoting-agent, interim writes

- Add tunnels table to state.db (schema v2→v3) with tunnel_type,
  pivot_host, target_subnet, local_endpoint, requires_proxychains, status
- Add tunnel MCP tools: get_tunnels (all modes), add_tunnel (interim+writer),
  update_tunnel (writer only); include Tunnels section in get_state_summary()
- Remove CHECK constraint from state_events.event_type to allow 'tunnel' events
- Create pivoting-agent (sonnet, skill-router + shell-server + state-interim)
  for tunnel setup via pivoting-tunneling skill with sudo handoff protocol
- Update all 5 discovery skills to use state-interim MCP with interim writes
  (add_credential, add_vuln, add_pivot, add_blocked) for real-time findings
- Add pivoting workflow to orchestrator: pivot-to-tunnel flow, tunnel context
  in agent prompts, tunnel health checks, pivoting-agent routing
- Update docs: CLAUDE.md, state-server README, orchestrator routing tables

commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Dashboard: only show active agents, auto-remove completed

Active agents are detected by output file mtime (60s threshold).
Completed agents are automatically removed from the dashboard.
Completed agents remain accessible via the agent browser (b key).
If no agents are active, the dashboard sits idle until one spawns.

commit.message:
Add agent browser, double-tap dismiss, and operator approval rule

Dashboard:
- Add agent browser (b key) — lists all agent transcripts from the
  session sorted by recency, with skill/agent-type labels extracted
  from JSONL content. Agents already in the dashboard shown with ●
  marker. Enter to add, Esc/b to close.
- Double-tap dismiss (d key) — first press shows "tap d again to
  dismiss" warning in yellow, second press within 2s actually removes
  the pane. Prevents accidental dismissals.
- Fix tasks directory path derivation in dashboard.sh — sed was
  stripping a leading dash that the actual directory keeps.
- Label extraction: detect Plan/Explore agents from prompt keywords,
  extract skill names from "Load skill" patterns, fall back to prompt
  summary instead of hex agent IDs.

Orchestrator:
- Add mandatory operator approval rule before every agent spawn.
  Presented as a top-level constraint alongside "don't run scanning
  tools". No agents launch without explicit operator sign-off.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: smooth tracer line with Chaikin algorithm + refine route coords

Applies Chaikin's corner-cutting algorithm (4 iterations) to the raw
GeoJSON coordinates at runtime, producing a smooth B-spline-like curve
through the control points without modifying the source file. The
moving point follows the same smoothed path. GeoJSON updated with
more precise coordinates traced from the actual street.

fix: weekly chart shows Mon-Sun of current week instead of rolling 7 days

rather than 6 days ago.

commit.message:
feat(profiles): full profile editor with environment variables

Replace the minimal name-only dialog with a full-screen editor that
allows setting environment variables (key-value pairs with show/hide
toggle), description, and an optional startup bash script — so users
can actually configure providers like MiniMax, Kimi Code, or DeepSeek
by setting ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN / ANTHROPIC_MODEL.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

commit.message:
fix: filter alertcenter scopes from user OAuth login flow (#108)

* fix: filter alertcenter scopes from user OAuth login flow

The `apps.alerts` scope is restricted to service accounts with
domain-wide delegation and fails with `400 invalid_scope` when
used in the standard 3-legged OAuth consent flow. Filter it out
alongside the existing chat.app/chat.bot/keep exclusions.

Fixes #73

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: also filter apps.alerts in is_app_only_scope()

The scope filter exists in two locations: setup.rs (fetch_scopes_for_apis)
and auth_commands.rs (is_app_only_scope). Both need the apps.alerts
exclusion to prevent it from appearing in the interactive scope picker.

commit.message:
Fix Plex AMD RDNA 4 (gfx12) hardware transcoding

The init container now:
- Uses cp -L (dereference) instead of cp -an (preserve symlinks) to
  avoid broken symlinks for libs like libelf-0.194.so
- Recursively resolves transitive deps via ldd (2-pass)
- Replaces Plex's cached VA-API driver with our Mesa 26 build, since
  PMS overrides LIBVA_DRIVERS_PATH to its own cache directory
- Mounts the config PVC to access the Plex driver cache

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add persist_run to all 50 protocol CLI runners

Every protocol's run.py now persists results to Postgres after execution,
with Langfuse trace_id linking. Persistence is best-effort (silently
no-ops if ce-db is unavailable).

commit.message:
Protect pipeline-queued issues from worktree GC collection (#1984)

The GC loop only checked active_issue_numbers and HITL state, missing
issues that were queued or in-flight in the IssueStore. This caused
worktrees to be destroyed for issues awaiting implementation, resulting
in zero-commit outcomes.

Adds is_in_pipeline() to IssueStore and wires it into WorktreeGCLoop
via a callback. Issues in any pipeline stage (queued, in-flight, active)
are now protected from GC.

Fixes #1982

Co-authored-by: T-rav-Hydra-Ops <t@koderex.dev>
